title: Radio Button Group
description: Radio Button Group renders a small single-choice set as button-like options with support for segmented or separate layout, orientation, icons, and per-option disabled state.
intro: Use `ui-radio-button-group` when users need to choose exactly one option from a small set and a button-like presentation fits better than classic radios. It works well for view switches, density controls, mode selectors, and compact settings choices.

featureSections.title: Basic single-choice groups
featureSections.description: The most common use case is a small set of mutually exclusive choices such as view mode or delivery speed.

featureSections.title: Layout and orientation
featureSections.description: Segmented horizontal groups feel like compact mode switches, while separate vertical groups work better when labels are longer or more descriptive.

featureSections.title: Icons, size, and emphasis
featureSections.description: Icons and stronger visual emphasis are useful when the choices are few, recognizable, and need to stand out as a quick control strip.

featureSections.title: Disabled, readonly, and required states
featureSections.description: State handling matters when some options are unavailable or when the selected value should stay visible without allowing changes.

featureSections.title: Settings panel composition
featureSections.description: A realistic radio-button group usually lives inside a compact settings or publishing panel rather than as a standalone control in empty space.

accessibility.title: Radiogroup semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-radio-button-group` renders a `radiogroup` and each option exposes radio semantics through button-based items with `role="radio"` and `aria-checked`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard handling follows the group orientation.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Moves focus into or out of the group. |
| ArrowLeft / ArrowRight | Moves selection in horizontal groups. |
| ArrowUp / ArrowDown | Moves selection in vertical groups. |

accessibility.title: Labels and disabled options
accessibility.body: Use a clear group label because the visible label is also the primary accessible name of the `radiogroup`. Disabled options should remain understandable rather than disappearing from the choice set.

apiSections.title: RadioButtonGroupComponent - ui-radio-button-group
apiSections.lead: Standalone single-choice button group built on top of the shared field system. It combines radio semantics with button presentation for compact mutually exclusive choices.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Visible label for the whole group.
Keeps the current value visible but prevents changes.
Choice set rendered by the group.
Direction of the rendered option list.
Whether buttons are visually connected or separated.
Corner treatment of the rendered buttons.

blocks.title: Outputs and item model
rows: Emits the selected value when the choice changes.
{ id, label, value, icon?, ariaLabel?, disabled? }

paragraphs: Use this component only for small single-choice sets. If the number of options grows or labels become long, a classic radio group, dropdown, or list pattern is usually clearer.
Segmented layout is strongest for quick mode switches. Separate layout is safer for more descriptive or vertically stacked choices.
